latest_mt076_supporting_evidence.summary: Implemented LlamaCppRuntime LoRA stack support for the current llama-cpp-2 0.1.146 safe wrapper: loaded llama.cpp models expose LoraStackOps, descriptor/base/sha/license validation runs before adapter activation, generation honors mounted lora_overrides on the per-request native context, unmounted overrides fail before decoding, KV-prefix plus LoRA is rejected because prefix handles are not LoRA-scoped, single active LoRA is enforced honestly, adapter lifetime is pinned to the owning native backend, and mount/unmount/swap do not restart the process. This is supporting implementation/test evidence only; WP remains In Progress and Integration Validator PASS remains pending.

supporting_reviews.summary: Confirmed locked llama-cpp-2 0.1.146 exposes only one safe LoRA adapter per context while upstream C supports multi-adapter arrays.

Found KV-prefix/LoRA race, missing stack mutation single-LoRA enforcement, native adapter lifetime leak/free risk, and negative-test gaps. Final implementation added apply-time KV-prefix rejection, mount/swap single-LoRA enforcement, explicit adapter free, and expanded tests.

Found post-fix native adapter/model drop-order risk and mount precheck ordering issue. Final implementation makes NativeLoraAdapter retain the owning NativeLlamaCppBackend and rejects second active mounts before adapter load.

Confirmed the correct governance state for MT-076 is completed implementation evidence with validator_verdict and integration_validator_verdict still PENDING, and WP ready_for_validation remains false.

residual_risks: No Integration Validator has reviewed MT-076; this record must not be treated as an authoritative WP pass.

True multi-LoRA on llama.cpp is not implemented under the locked safe Rust wrapper. Supporting it later requires a wrapper patch/fork or a safe upstream API for llama_set_adapters_lora with multiple handles.

No real LoRA fixture was configured in this session. Native load/generate tests exercised a real GGUF model, but real LoRA mount/application/unload negative paths remain env-gated on HANDSHAKE_TEST_LORA_PATH.

FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-LORA-MOUNT / UNMOUNT emission is not wired in the current ModelRuntime LoRA surface; no existing model-runtime event hook was present to reuse during this MT. Validator should decide whether this belongs in a later event-ledger MT or requires reopening this scope.
Native adapter free relies on locked llama-cpp-2 0.1.146 layout knowledge. If the crate later adds Drop for LlamaLoraAdapter or changes representation, this manual free path must be revisited.

latest_mt077_supporting_evidence.summary: MT-077 is locally implementation-complete as supporting evidence, not Integration Validator PASS. The erroneous red-team LlamaCppServer ledger clause was repaired to match the current master/refinement architecture: LlamaCppRuntime wraps llama-cpp-2 in-process, draft-model speculation uses already-loaded in-process LlamaCppRuntime handles, and no fake ProcessOwnershipLedger rows are emitted. Fresh process-ledger, native/default speculative, FR schema, static search, and fmt evidence all exit 0.

focused_hardening_suite.note: MT-077 now has contract-repair evidence plus process-ledger, native/default speculative, FR schema, static-search, and fmt proofs. Earlier hardening logs remain recorded.

residual_risks: No Integration Validator has reviewed MT-077; this record must not be treated as an authoritative pass.

A real llama-server subprocess backend is not implemented in MT-077. Official llama.cpp docs expose speculative decoding through llama-server CLI/env/preset configuration; if Handshake wants that server-owned process architecture, it needs a separate dependency MT with SandboxAdapter spawn, START/STOP ledger rows, health checks, metrics/log capture, and request routing.

LlamaCppRuntime emits FR-EVT-LLM-INFER-SPEC-ACCEPT/REJECT only when a FlightRecorder is injected and a Tokio runtime handle is available. Mitigation: product call sites that require persistent observability must construct the runtime with a recorder.

GenerateRequest has no caller-supplied trace/correlation id, so speculative FR events currently use a runtime-minted generation trace_id. Mitigation: add a request trace field in a future observability MT if cross-surface correlation is required.

Separate-draft live proof remains env-gated on HANDSHAKE_TEST_DRAFT_GGUF_PATH; this host only proved same-GGUF draft-model and ngram paths with the configured local GGUF. Mitigation: configure a compatible distinct draft GGUF and rerun the native suite when available.

Draft tokenizer compatibility is checked per generation and may become expensive for large vocabularies. Mitigation: later cache a tokenizer compatibility fingerprint per loaded target/draft pair if profiling shows request-start overhead.
latest_mt077_supporting_evidence.caveat: Supporting implementation/test/contract-repair evidence only. MT-077 is locally completed for Kernel Builder with Integration Validator verdict still PENDING; no authoritative WP pass is implied.

latest_mt078_supporting_evidence.summary: MT-078 LlamaCppRuntime score/embed implementation is READY_FOR_VALIDATION, not COMPLETED. score() now validates token ids, decodes source tokens, computes next-token log probabilities, and returns the live Score mean_logprob shape. embed() now decodes embedding-enabled contexts, returns deterministic finite vectors, and fails closed with CapabilityNotSupported when embeddings are disabled. Native crash hardening records that forcing Mean pooling and encode-first produced STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION on the configured generative GGUF; final implementation uses llama.cpp model-default pooling and last-token embedding fallback for generative models.
latest_mt078_supporting_evidence.contract_drift: The MT contract prose says total_logprob/model_id/dim, while live product types expose mean_logprob and vector-only embedding. Implementation follows live shared trait/types; this is recorded as contract drift for validator review.

latest_mt091_supporting_evidence.summary: MT-091 implemented the LoRA stack composer surface as a React TSX component (contract said Svelte; same Svelte->TSX defect class as MT-098/102/105/124). Backed by a new app/src/lib/ipc/lora.ts IPC binding wrapping kernel_model_runtime_lora_{mount,unmount,swap,list} with camelCase DTOs and client-side strength range validation. Composer renders only when capabilities.supportsLora === true (hidden, not greyed), shows an ordered list with per-row license badge, strength slider 0..2 step 0.05, unmount button, drag-to-reorder via HTML5 native drag events, a mount-from-disk dialog (Tauri plugin-dialog file picker + descriptor form), and a Save-as-Work-Profile button. All mutations dispatch through the MT-090 Tauri commands, which preflight ModelRuntimeState.lora_command_binding via KernelActionCatalogV1.
contract_drift: Owned files listed Svelte components; app stack is React/TSX. Implemented as TSX + a new IPC binding (React hooks subsume the Svelte store role).
Proof command listed `pnpm run typecheck` which does not exist in app/package.json; substituted with `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`.
Proof command listed Playwright; inference_lab test pattern is Vitest + @testing-library/react. Implemented as Vitest unit tests covering the minimum_controls and adversarial scenarios.

blocker_quality_assessment.architectural_mt068_mt096: HONEST BLOCKER. The 8 cluster C UI/steering/config MTs (MT-098-105) genuinely cannot satisfy spec requirements without runtime-manager wiring through Tauri commands owned by MT-068 + MT-096. MT-068 and MT-096 are lifecycle.status=COMPLETED but their product code returns live_runtime_unavailable from every dispatch. Per operator-imposed scope constraint (no_claims_outside_27_until_resolved=true), implementer cannot modify those files. Operator must reopen MT-068+MT-096 or explicitly waive the no-claims-outside-27 rule.
blocker_quality_assessment.llvm_libclang_mt106: OVERSTATED BLOCKER. MT-106 abliteration requires weight-orthogonalisation producing GGUF/safetensors artifacts. The current blocker names llama-cpp-sys-2 (requires LLVM/libclang) as the only path. However, Candle's safetensors I/O is a real alternative documented in abliterate.rs's own module docstring ('safetensors-compatible candle build') and aligned with spec §3.6.4 ExternalEngineImport precedent. Candle deps require no host LLVM. A future implementer session should reattempt MT-106 via Candle path before requiring LLVM install.
blocker_quality_assessment.build_broken_17_mts: FALSE BLOCKER — RETRACTED. The autonomous session prematurely BLOCKED 17 MTs after misdiagnosing the build failure as catastrophic. The MT-127 validator (VALIDATOR-EXPLORE-MT127-20260520-AUTO) explicitly retracted this diagnosis: the actual fix was 5 pub mod declarations in lib.rs (model_manual, model_runtime, models, process_ledger, sandbox, test_harness) plus reverting sandbox_binding.rs to crate::sandbox. After the fix, cargo check exits clean (50 warnings, 0 errors). MT-127 closure proved the build is fixable in-scope. These 17 BLOCKED dispositions are valid lifecycle records of the implementer's session-time blocker state, but they should be re-evaluated by a future session against the now-buildable feat branch (which is committed in 991683da).

operator_action_menu.option_a_reopen_mt_068_mt_096: Reopen MT-068 + MT-096 with full reimplementation authority. Cost: extends reopened set from 27 to 29 MTs. Effect: unblocks MT-098-105 cluster C UI/steering chain.
operator_action_menu.option_b_waive_no_claims_outside_27_for_: Explicitly waive no_claims_outside_27_until_resolved=true for the narrow scope of runtime-manager wiring in MT-068/MT-096 owned files. operator_action_menu.option_c_evaluate_candle_safetensors_pat: Before requiring LLVM install, direct future session to implement MT-106 abliteration via Candle's safetensors I/O path (already documented in abliterate.rs and aligned with spec §3.6.4). Cost: no operator action. Effect: potentially unblocks MT-106 without host LLVM install.
operator_action_menu.option_d_re_evaluate_17_build_broken_mts: Direct future session to re-claim the 17 MTs that were prematurely BLOCKED on the misdiagnosed build break. The build is now confirmed clean (cargo check exits 0; MT-127 live test passes; see commit 991683da on feat branch). Cost: future session capacity. Effect: likely closures for memory_capsule chain (MT-141-147 — prior session reported PASS counts against the working build) and BYOK cloud lanes (MT-125-126-128 via Wiremock per operator clarification block). Distillation chain (MT-119-124) additionally needs Python peft/transformers/torch install.

operator_action_menu.option_f_authorize_llvm_install_for_mt10: Authorize LLVM/libclang install for MT-074 chain. Heaviest path. Only do this after Option C (Candle safetensors) is evaluated and rejected.

risks: R-1 Scope-consuming-product: This is the largest WP in project history. A WP this large risks the same failure mode the reset brief escaped, where the harness consumes the product.
R-2 PRODUCTION-tier research-frontier risk: Subquadratic full feature parity is research-frontier work; per RW-3 the implementation is sized by feature_parity_detail items rather than calendar time.
R-3 Sandbox-architecture-flip-orphans-KERNEL-003: 3-adapter pattern preserves DockerAdapter; migration MTs map existing KERNEL-003 sandbox consumers.
R-4 Spec-enrichment-becomes-its-own-project: Resolved by single coherent v02.186 bundle bump (no incremental drift).

R-5 LM-Studio/Ollama-lockout: Mitigated by ExternalEngineImport lane (spec Â§3.6.4) that lets operator point Handshake at a model file path or local OpenAI-compatible endpoint without depending on those apps as runtime authority.
R-6 Visual-debugger-parallel-mutation-path: Mitigated by 3-layer parallel-mutation controls (compile-time trait separation + runtime localhost+secret+feature-flag + audit log per spec Â§6.5).

R-7 Self-improvement-overfit: Mitigated by 60/20/20 train/dev/holdout split + multi-metric promotion floor + Goodhart sentinel + HBR-SWARM-002 loop counter + PromotionGate operator review (spec Â§4.8.3).
R-8 MoD-Subquad-feasibility: MoD resolved by spec-deferring to Phase 3 roadmap (Â§6.8); Subquadratic locked at full feature parity per operator E-2.
R-9 FEMS-V1-API-drift: B-FEMS-V1-INTEGRATION-SURFACE blocker; verification pass in Stage 4D before cluster D MT authoring.
R-10 WindowsNativeJailAdapter-crate-license: License audit MT required before adopting codex-windows-sandbox or rappct crate.
R-11 Process-ledger-perf-under-swarm: Mitigated by batched async writes + per-process metadata cap + async write path + FR-EVT-LEDGER-OVERFLOW degraded mode (spec Â§5.7.3).
R-12 mojibake-in-spec-module-04: Pre-existing UTF-8 double-encoding in legacy Â§4.2 content; Stage 2 subagent worked around it; cleanup is non-blocking governance debt.
R-13 AC-cross-reference-drift: 52 AC-* IDs in spec cross-referenced from refinement.json; renames or splits must update both surfaces in same change.

lanes.rationale: WP-KERNEL-004 is a two-lane deliverable: the HBR-Enforcement suffix in the WP name requires authoring both the HBR governance gates AND the product features those gates validate. The gate scripts and rule registry live in .GOV/ (governance lane) and the gated features live in src/ + app/ (product lane). Both lanes are intentionally in this packet's scope because each is unbuildable without the other: gates have nothing to validate without the features, and the features have no enforceable contract without the gates. Per [CX-503P] self-hosting convergence, the .GOV/ HBR gates will eventually migrate into product code; at that point the .GOV/ allow_paths entries become dead globs that mt-packet-scope-alignment-check will surface as INFO so the operator can prune them.

product_lane.description: Handshake Kernel V1 product code: sandbox + adapters, model runtime + adapters, process ledger, HBR-gated product features (visual debug, focus-quiet runtime, swarm harness, model manual, operator foreground/inspector surfaces), distillation, memory, self-improve, kernel call sites, role mailbox, observability, llm_client, flight_recorder. Tests under tests/ and src/.../tests/. App-side: Tauri command surface, Svelte/TS IPC + stores + diagnostic/inference-lab/model-runtime components, root app config (App.tsx/App.css/playwright.config.ts), root tooling config (clippy.toml), helper scripts (scripts/distill/**).

governance_lane.description: HBR rule registry + gate scripts + checks + schemas + visual baselines + role protocol linkage + governance templates + sandbox audit records + FR event registry. These are the deterministic enforcement layer that makes HBR rules meaningful for this WP and every subsequent Handshake build. They live in .GOV/ today; per [CX-503P] they migrate into product code when Handshake self-governs.

scope.allowed_paths_matcher_note: The .GOV/roles_shared/{scripts,checks,schemas}/** and .GOV/roles/** entries are broader than the actual HBR-specific files this WP touches. The narrower forms (.GOV/roles_shared/scripts/hbr-*, .GOV/roles/**/PROTOCOL.md, etc.) are not supported by scope-surface-lib's matchesScopeEntry which strips only /**, /**/*, /* suffixes — file-prefix and **/file.ext patterns silently fail to match. Follow-up RGF could enhance the shared matcher to handle file-prefix globs and brace expansion; until then, the directory-broad form is the accurate scope representation for this WP. Pre-commit and post-commit checks use the same matcher (per F-004) so this is consistent across the gate boundary.

scope.summary: KERNEL-004 lands the Kernel-V0-Substrate four-cluster max-fold: (A) HBR enforcement at build+handoff gate + visual debugger + backend inspector plane + non-hijacking GUI invariants + swarm-agent harness + ModelManual currency + Codex CX-131 linkage; (B) SandboxAdapter trait with 3 adapters (WSL2+Podman default, WindowsNativeJailAdapter strict, Docker compat); (C) ModelRuntime + LocalModelAdapter (LlamaCppRuntime default + CandleRuntime for hook-requiring techniques + subquadratic) + 8 PRODUCTION inference techniques + Distillation pipeline + MoD spec-deferred per Phase 3 roadmap; (D) Memory V0+ MemoryCapsule retrieval + Karpathy-style self-improvement loop on fixed ~30-item HBR test-packet corpus on top of FEMS V1.
scope.why: Each cluster on its own would touch the same kernel, sandbox, model, memory, and HBR surfaces. Splitting them into separate WPs would force three or more sequential migrations of the same product code, each one redoing the spec enrichment, the activation review, and the sandbox/runtime choice. The max-fold is operator-locked (2026-05-18) because the architecture decisions in clusters B and C and the enforcement decisions in cluster A are mutually entangled. KERNEL-001 (EventLedger/SessionBroker/dummy ModelAdapter/ToolGate/ArtifactStore/PromotionGate/TraceProjection), KERNEL-002 (KernelActionCatalogV1/WriteBoxV1/CRDT workspace), KERNEL-003 (Sandbox/ValidationRunner/PromotionGate), and FEMS V1 (typed memory + session integration + operator panel + flight-recorder events + bounded memory pack injection + review-gated writes) are the stable foundation; KERNEL-004 builds on them.
